description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, resulting in the largest land war in Europe since World War II. The conflict has severely disrupted global energy markets, grain exports, and fertilizer supply chains. Ukraine and Russia together accounted for nearly 30% of global wheat exports before the war.

deficiencies.detail: Acute food insecurity for 6M+ people in conflict zones
Frequent blackouts; 40% of energy infrastructure destroyed
500+ hospitals damaged; medicine supply chain broken
3M+ people without reliable water access

challenges: Widespread destruction of civilian infrastructure including power grids, water systems, and hospitals
14.6 million people internally displaced, the largest displacement crisis in Europe in decades
Severe shortages of heating fuel during winter months, causing hypothermia risks
Agricultural land contaminated with landmines, reducing future food production capacity
Economic collapse in eastern regions with GDP contraction exceeding 35%
Mental health crisis with PTSD affecting an estimated 4 million civilians

description: Russia, as the aggressor in the Ukraine conflict, faces comprehensive international sanctions that have restructured global energy markets. Despite sanctions, Russia continues to export oil and gas at discounted prices to China, India, and Turkey, reshaping global trade flows. Russia is the world's second-largest oil exporter and largest natural gas exporter.

deficiencies.detail: Shortage of imported electronics, medicines, and automotive parts
Semiconductor export ban causing aviation and industrial slowdowns
Ruble volatility; 40% inflation at peak
SWIFT exclusion limiting international financial transactions

challenges: Brain drain with over 500,000 skilled workers emigrating since 2022
Military-industrial complex consuming civilian budget, reducing public services
Technological stagnation due to semiconductor and dual-use technology bans
Inflation reaching 17% in 2022, severely impacting household purchasing power
Isolation from global financial systems creating long-term economic damage

description: Following the Hamas attack on October 7, 2023, Israel launched extensive military operations in Gaza. The conflict has created a severe humanitarian crisis and raised regional instability concerns, particularly around Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping — a critical global trade artery carrying 12% of world trade and 30% of global container traffic.

shippingRoutes.name: Red Sea / Suez Canal Route
shippingRoutes.impact: 70% traffic rerouted via Cape of Good Hope; +14 days transit

deficiencies.detail: IPC Phase 5 Famine conditions in northern Gaza
Only 4% of pre-war water production available
Only 14 out of 36 hospitals partially functional
Complete fuel blockade halting generators and ambulances

challenges: Over 1.9 million people displaced into Rafah with nowhere to evacuate
Complete blockade preventing food, water, fuel, and medicine from entering northern Gaza
Famine declared by IPC in northern Gaza — first famine declaration in the 21st century Middle East
Healthcare system collapse with insufficient supplies to treat mass casualty wounds
Children comprising over 40% of casualties, highest rate of child deaths in modern conflict history
No clean water access for 2.3 million civilians; cholera and dysentery outbreak risk

status: Active — Houthi escalation targeting Red Sea
description: Yemen's decade-long civil war has created the world's worst humanitarian crisis. Since October 2023, Houthi forces have dramatically escalated attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden in solidarity with Gaza, forcing global shippers to reroute around the Cape of Good Hope, significantly increasing shipping times and costs.

shippingRoutes.name: Bab el-Mandeb Strait
shippingRoutes.impact: Missile and drone attacks on commercial vessels

shippingRoutes.name: Gulf of Aden
shippingRoutes.impact: Major shipping lines avoiding route entirely

deficiencies.detail: 17 million people facing acute food insecurity
Critical fuel shortage halting water pumps and hospitals
50% of health facilities non-functional
Economic collapse with 80% poverty rate

challenges: 21.6 million people — over two-thirds of the population — require humanitarian assistance
Cholera, dengue, and COVID have swept through displacement camps due to lack of clean water
Fuel shortages cause hospitals, water pumps, and bakeries to stop functioning
Blockade of Hudaydah port has prevented critical food and medicine imports
Children suffering from severe acute malnutrition at scale
Economy destroyed; Yemeni Rial has lost 80% of its value

description: Sudan erupted into full-scale civil war in April 2023 between the national army and a powerful paramilitary force. The conflict has produced the world's largest displacement crisis as of 2024, with mass atrocities reported in Darfur. Sudan's oil production and export infrastructure has been severely damaged.

shippingRoutes.name: Port Sudan Export Routes
shippingRoutes.impact: Oil pipeline to Port Sudan damaged

deficiencies.detail: Famine declared in Zamzam camp; 755,000 in catastrophic food insecurity
70-80% of health facilities non-operational in conflict zones
Mass displacement with no WASH access in camps
Mass atrocities, sexual violence used as weapon of war in Darfur

challenges: World's largest displacement crisis in 2024 with over 10.7 million people displaced
Famine declared in Zamzam displacement camp in North Darfur — first declared famine since 2017
Mass atrocities and ethnic cleansing reported in Darfur echoing the 2003-2008 genocide
Humanitarian access blocked; aid organizations unable to reach millions in need
Complete economic collapse with hyperinflation exceeding 200%
Children out of school for over a year across conflict-affected states

description: Following the 2021 military coup, Myanmar descended into civil war. The military junta faces growing resistance from ethnic armed groups and the People's Defence Force. Myanmar's rare earth minerals, natural gas exports to China and Thailand, and agricultural exports have all been severely disrupted.

deficiencies.detail: Conflict-induced food insecurity for 12.9 million people
Systematic attacks on healthcare workers; 500+ incidents
National grid failures; fuel price inflation 200%
School system collapsed; 600,000+ children out of school

challenges: Military attacks on civilian populations including airstrikes on villages and displacement camps
Systematic destruction of healthcare infrastructure; doctors arrested for treating wounded
Currency collapse with Kyat losing 60% of value against USD
Internet shutdowns preventing communication and access to information
18.6 million people in need of humanitarian assistance with severely limited aid access

description: Eastern DR Congo has been in conflict for nearly three decades. The 2024 M23 rebel offensive has seized the city of Goma and surrounding regions rich in cobalt, coltan, and cassiterite — minerals critical to global electric vehicle batteries and electronics supply chains. DR Congo supplies 70% of the world's cobalt.

deficiencies.detail: 26 million acutely food insecure; IPC Phase 3+ across eastern provinces
Cholera, mpox, and ebola outbreaks compounding conflict-related injuries
Mass sexual violence documented; armed group recruitment of children
7.2M IDPs with inadequate shelter in overcrowded camps

challenges: Largest ongoing humanitarian crisis in Africa with 23 million in need
Children recruited as soldiers by all armed factions including government-allied militias
Mineral wealth extraction driving conflict rather than funding development
Cholera and mpox epidemics spreading through displacement camps with no medical infrastructure
Aid workers and hospitals systematically targeted by armed groups
Humanitarian funding gap exceeding 70% of assessed needs

status: Fragile Ceasefire — Amhara region still active
description: The Tigray War, one of the deadliest conflicts of the 21st century, devastated northern Ethiopia from 2020-2022. A ceasefire was reached in November 2022 but fighting continues in the Amhara region. Ethiopia's agricultural exports, coffee, and cut flowers trade remain severely disrupted. Ethiopia is Africa's second most populous nation.

shippingRoutes.name: Djibouti Port Access Road
shippingRoutes.impact: Land route disruptions adding 3-5 days to shipments

deficiencies.detail: Famine conditions reported in Tigray; aid access only partially restored
Tigray healthcare system 70% destroyed during conflict
UN documented mass sexual violence as a systematic weapon of war
Agricultural land systematically destroyed; farming communities starved

challenges: Post-war reconstruction of Tigray requires an estimated $20 billion over a decade
Continued Amhara conflict destabilizing the second-largest state
Mass atrocities documentation ongoing; no accountability mechanism established
Agricultural communities unable to replant due to destroyed irrigation and tools
An estimated 500,000 people died in the Tigray war according to UN estimates
20.1 million people still requiring humanitarian assistance despite ceasefire
